title: rootless: allow to override configuration files

comments.body: Isn't this a WIP since it is waiting for storage PR?
yes, changing the PR title

I am not fully convinced of the way `mounts.conf` is looked-up.

If an user defines a mounts.conf, should we still look into `/etc/containers/mounts.conf` and `/usr/share/containers/mounts.conf` (as the code does now) or should we skip these two files instead?

The code is only supposed to read one of them, I believe.

If /etc/containers/mounts.conf exists then don't read /usr/share/containers/mounts.conf.

So I guess if the user creates a local ~/.local/containers/mounts.conf, only that file would be read.

we currently read both `/etc/containers/mounts.conf` and `/usr/share/containers/mounts.conf`, i.e. we don't stop on the first found but we parse both.  Now I am adding a third one, and this PR follows the same logic of what was done previously so we end up with parsing 3 files and adding all the mounts of these files.

I also don't like much this logic, I think there the highest precedence file should override completely the previous ones.

Agree. Parsing all three seems unnecessary. The rest of our config files
that follow this pattern stop at the first file they encounter.

I added a patch to change the logic, now the lookup stops on the first file that exists.
seems like the CI is broken, I am seeing here the same failures as on the other PR
@giuseppe Agree, seems like a general problem with papr
tests are finally happy
LGTM, although I too pref @rhatdan's suggested message.
